metaTitle: Cardiovascular Health & Cholesterol | Piedmont Nutrition Care
metaDescription: Lower LDL, raise HDL, reduce triglycerides through targeted food chemistry. Cardiovascular nutrition therapy in Winston-Salem, NC.
headline: Your cholesterol numbers don't have to keep heading the wrong direction. The right foods can help.
subheadline: High cholesterol and cardiovascular risk don't have to mean a lifetime of medication alone. Piedmont Nutrition Care uses your lab data to build a targeted food plan that supports your heart health from the inside out — with foods you'll actually enjoy eating.
agitation: Your numbers keep going up despite doing what you were told — eating less fat, taking your statin, trying to exercise more. It can feel like your body isn't cooperating, but the truth is the approach may need to be more specific to you.
Heart health is deeply connected to what you eat — not just avoiding "bad" foods, but understanding which specific foods support your cardiovascular system based on your unique bloodwork and biology.
howItWorks: Jeanne reviews your full lipid panel — not just total cholesterol, but the particle sizes, ratios, and inflammatory markers that actually predict cardiovascular risk. She identifies which dietary patterns are driving your specific lipid profile and builds a protocol that targets those drivers directly.

You'll learn which foods lower LDL through real mechanisms (not marketing claims), how to raise HDL through food choices, and which so-called "heart-healthy" products are actually making things worse.

testimonial.quote: Jeanne is extremely knowledgeable about food, food chemistry, and the latest research findings on nutrition. She can suggest dietary changes to prevent cancer, boost a sluggish metabolism, improve blood sugar, or lower LDL.
testimonial.author: Liz

ctaTriggers: Walk into your next appointment with confidence
subheadlineShort: High cholesterol doesn't have to mean medication alone. We build a food plan that supports your heart health with foods you'll enjoy.
agitationShort: Your numbers keep rising despite following advice. The approach may need to be more specific to your body.
Heart health is about more than avoiding "bad" foods — it's understanding which foods support your cardiovascular system.
howItWorksShort: Jeanne analyzes your full lipid panel, inflammatory markers, and metabolic indicators to identify what's actually driving your cardiovascular risk.
Your protocol targets the specific mechanisms — not just "eat less fat" but which fats reduce inflammation and which foods optimize your lipid ratios.
